commit.message: feat: parallel tool execution, session history 30, image display, disconnect fix - Parallel tool execution: multiple tool calls in a single LLM iteration now run concurrently via FreeRTOS tasks (PSRAM output buffers per call), results collected in call order; sequential fallback on OOM - Session history: LANG_AGENT_MAX_HISTORY and LANG_SESSION_MAX_MSGS 15→30, session file size cap doubled to 12KB - capture_image image display: ws_server_send_with_tts gains optional image_url param; agent tracks capture_image calls and includes /camera/latest.jpg URL in response; index.html renders below the assistant bubble (click to zoom) - WebSocket disconnects: max_open_sockets 4→8 to handle concurrent API + WS connections without hitting the socket limit - Daily notes (memory_append_today) and vision tool (capture_image) confirmed already fully implemented and registered Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6

refactor: extract map-view component + update env example

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: stuck detection uses progress bar value, not heading

Heading is always "Apply to <Company>" on every step — comparing it
caused false stuck detection on step 2. Now:
- Progress bar selector finds <progress> elements (no explicit role)
- Stuck detection re-reads progress AFTER clicking Next to see if it changed
- Threshold raised to 3 same-progress clicks before exiting

commit.message:
fix: enclave invite by email via profiles table, fix unknown owner display

- Add migration 006: email column on profiles, populated from auth.users
- Sync user email to profiles on every dashboard login
- loadEnclaveData now fetches profiles separately (avoids RLS chain)
  and attaches them to each member row — fixes "Unknown" display name
- Init enclave restore uses loadEnclaveData instead of inline join
- inviteMember already queries profiles.email (Option A) — now works

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: report card logo, PDF generation, and print quality

ReportCard:
- Default logo now uses /images/logo.png instead of 'R' gradient
- Removed brightness-0 invert CSS filter that was hiding logo colors
- onError fallback to /images/logo.png if uploaded logo fails to load
- crossOrigin=anonymous on logo and student photo for html2canvas
- Width changed from 210mm to 794px (exact A4 pixel width) — fixes
  html2canvas pixel mapping; fonts and layout no longer get clipped

PDF generation (builder/page.tsx):
- Hidden capture div moved from absolute left:-9999px to fixed opacity:0
  (still in viewport — html2canvas can now actually render it)
- Pre-loads all img elements before calling html2canvas so photos
  and logos are captured correctly
- 120ms settle delay after React render before capture
- jsPDF now uses px units with exact canvas dimensions — no more
  scaling artifacts or clipped content
- Better error message shown in UI instead of alert()

Preview modal:
- Removed 0.85 scale transform (was distorting preview)
- Report now scrollable at true 794px width

Add AI Content Studio for visual content creation

Full-stack implementation of Content Studio accessible from Content Calendar:

  OpenRouter Gemini 2.5 Pro, FFmpeg video rendering, unified publishing

  scene editor/templates), CSS animation system, media uploader, scene editor
  with background/text/overlay controls, 4 pre-built templates (matchday,
  victory, player spotlight, quote card), html-to-image PNG export, publish
  modal with platform integration

Combine SoldiAPI + website into single Docker container

Embeds the Python SoldiAPI (FastAPI odds scraper) directly into the
soldi-website repo so both services run in one Render deployment.
Uses supervisord to manage both Node.js (port 3000) and Python
(port 3001) processes. Internal communication via localhost.

All Playwright-based scrapers remain disabled via DISABLED_SCRAPERS
to stay within free-tier memory limits (512MB).

server components.

Add comprehensive reward modeling theory documentation

Created docs/REWARD_MODELING_THEORY.md covering Phase 3 foundations:

Theory & Concepts:
- What reward modeling is and why it's needed
- Bradley-Terry model mathematical formulation
- Loss function derivation and intuition
- Comparison with direct human feedback

Preference Data:
- Data format and structure
- 5 detailed examples across different categories:
  * Quality comparison
  * Helpfulness
  * Safety
  * Truthfulness
  * Conciseness
- Real-world datasets (Anthropic HH-RLHF, OpenAssistant, SHP)
- Data collection process

Training Process:
- Step-by-step training pipeline
- Architecture (LM + value head → scalar)
- Hyperparameters and best practices
- What the model learns

Evaluation Metrics:
- Ranking accuracy (primary metric)
- Reward margin
- Calibration
- Reward distributions
- Cross-category performance

RLHF Integration:
- How reward models fit in RLHF pipeline
- Preview of Phase 5 (PPO)
- Comparison with DPO (Phase 4)

Implementation Strategy:
- Phase 3 roadmap
- Component breakdown
- Testing strategy
- Success criteria

Includes working pseudocode, examples, and intuitive explanations.
Prepares for Phase 3 implementation starting with reward model architecture.

commit.message:
Fix env var priority and empty-string handling in _build_environment_variables

Three fixes:
1. cloud_context_env: use explicit None check and str() coerce so dicts with
   empty-string values are applied correctly (was using truthiness check on dict).
2. containerContext bare keys: don't clobber a real value from cloud_context_env
   with an empty default when the agent doesn't have the key. Only fall back to ""
   when nothing has set the key yet.
3. Empty-string default: bare keys not in agent env AND not set by any earlier layer
   now get "" rather than being omitted, so dg.EnvVar() resolves without
   PostProcessingError at ExecutionPlanSnapshot time.

feat: auth layer, plugin registry, settings panel, welcome screen, context compaction

- REST endpoints check Authorization header or ?token= query param
- POST /api/auth/verify for web UI token validation
- GET /api/health with uptime, plugin counts, session count
- Plugin registry (persistent ~/.bogs/plugins.json) tracks all plugins ever connected
- GET /api/registry returns active + historical plugins
- bogs init now prompts for access token (auto-generate or custom)
- bogs token show/reset/clear commands
- Conversation compaction: summarizes and drops old messages when history exceeds 80k chars
- Web UI: AuthScreen with token entry when server has auth enabled
- Web UI: SettingsPanel (model, API key, clear sessions) behind gear icon
- Web UI: WelcomeScreen with suggestion chips on empty sessions
- Web UI: PluginBar registry dropdown with connection history
- All fetch calls pass Authorization header when token is set
